item.title: Jared Kushner, Steve Witkoff and the Profitable Business of Peace
item.description: The Trump administration is turning diplomacy into an asset to be leveraged.

item.title: Takeaways From The Times’s Interview With Tucker Carlson
item.description: He spoke about the rupture with President Trump and what it portends for the conservative movement. Here are the key moments from our conversation.

item.title: What Does Tucker Carlson Really Believe? I Went to Maine to Find Out.
item.description: The conservative media commentator split with the administration over the war in Iran. Will the breakup last?

item.title: A Homeless Person’s Pet Needed Help. Should I Have Tried to Buy It?
item.description: I could tell that the man loved his cat, but also that he was unable to provide proper care for it.

item.title: I Regret to Inform You of the Better Face Inside Your Face
item.description: The world has gone crazy for tools and tricks that slim the visage. Is vanity to blame — or something weirder?

item.title: Argentina’s Leader, a MAGA Celebrity, Is in a Race Against Time to Prove Himself
item.description: With his signature chain saw, Javier Milei made himself a symbol of a global movement. At home, his state-slashing experiment is on the line.

item.title: The Longevity Secrets Helping Athletes Blow Past the Limits of Age
item.description: With cutting-edge sports medicine and sci-fi gadgetry, more and more athletes are figuring out how to extend their careers.

item.title: Can I Ask My Brother to Have His Racist Prison Tattoo Removed?
item.description: I know that some tattoo artists will cover white-supremacist tattoos at no cost for those who have disavowed such views.

item.title: Can You Sneak Chocolate Chips From the Pantry?
item.description: A ruling on a dispute over purloined baking ingredients.

item.title: Gisèle Pelicot’s Memoir Said Something Taboo About Victimhood. We Didn’t Listen.
item.description: A book by the world’s most famous survivor of sexual violence has been read as a manifesto or a cry of pain. What she wrote is far more complicated.

item.title: A Personal Finance Star on What Millennials Need From Their Boomer Parents
item.description: Ramit Sethi wants everyone to have a healthier relationship to money, and thinks he knows how to get us there.

item.title: He Thinks Netflix Accused Him of Murder. The Courts Disagree.
item.description: When the famed freediver Francisco Ferreras filed a libel suit over “No Limit,” his case looked strong. Proving it would require a more honest accounting of his life.

item.title: What if You Could Give Your Dog a Longer Life?
item.description: The business of pet longevity is booming — driven, in part, by experimental treatments that might also have implications for us.

item.title: The Astounding Discovery That Could Link Eastern and Western Medicine
item.description: The detection of another circulatory system in the human body could have enormous scientific implications.
